title: World Cup 2026: Julián Quiñones, Raúl Jiménez lead Mexico to dominant 2-0 win over South Africa
original_text:
World Cup 2026: Julián Quiñones, Raúl Jiménez lead Mexico to dominant 2-0 win over South Africa
The 2026 World Cup kicked off on Thursday, and the defending champion from the 2022 tournament, Argentina, is set to open its campaign against Algeria on Tuesday in Group J, which also includes Austria and Jordan.
The Argentinian squad for the 2026 World Cup looks very similar to the 2022 team, which took down France in the final to win its third title in history. One of, if not the biggest play from that final was a save made by Emiliano Martinez on a shot from Randal Kolo Muani
in the 123rd minute with the game tied 3-3.
Advertisement
Martinez's save ensured the game went to penalty kicks, in which Argentina triumphed over France, 4-2.
Argentina goalkeeper Emiliano Martinez (23) against France in the 2022 World Cup final.© Yukihito Taguchi-Imagn Images
Heading into this year's World Cup, Martinez, who plays for Aston Villa in the English Premier League, was dealing with a fracture in the ring finger on his right hand, which he sustained during warm-ups before the Europa League Final in May.
On Thursday, Argentinian journalist Gaston Edul reported that Martinez got his test results back and they were positive, meaning he will be ready for his team's first game in the World Cup next week.
Advertisement
"Dibu Martínez got tested and the results were good," Edul said. "He had the cast removed. He's going to keep the finger protected but he can put on the gloves. He's going to start demanding it. Everything's proceeding according to what they planned. He's going to play goalkeeper against Algeria."
Dibu Martínez se hizo estudios y le dio bien. Se sacó el yeso. Va a seguir con el dedo protegido pero puede ponerse los guantes. Lo va a empezar a exigir. Sigue todo según lo que planificaron. Va a atajar contra Argelia.
The media could not be played.
Reload
This is major news for Argentina fans, who were worried their hero from 2022 was not going to be able to play this summer.
Despite having to keep his finger protected, Martinez will be on the pitch, alongside stars like Lionel Messi, Julian Alvarez and Lautaro Martinez when Argentina takes on Algeria in Kansas City on Tuesday.
